sentences.zh: 武帝立十八年，為前將軍，與匈奴戰，敗，降匈奴。

translations.literal: When eighteen years had passed since Emperor Wu’s accession, he was van general, fought the Xiongnu, was defeated, and surrendered to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Eighteen years into Emperor Wu’s reign he led the van against the Xiongnu, was beaten, and went over to the enemy.

sentences.zh: 趙食其

translations.literal: Zhao Shiyi (subsection heading).
translations.idiomatic: The following subsection treats Zhao Shiyi.

sentences.zh: 趙食其，祋祤人。

translations.literal: Zhao Shiyi was a man of Dihu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Shiyi came from Dihu.

sentences.zh: 武帝立十八年，以主爵都尉從大將軍，斬首六百六十級。

translations.literal: When eighteen years had passed since Emperor Wu’s accession, as chief commandant for nobility he followed the great general and beheaded 660 grades.
translations.idiomatic: Eighteen years into the reign he followed the great general as chief commandant for nobility and logged six hundred sixty counted kills.

sentences.zh: 元狩三年，賜爵關內侯，黃金百斤。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yuanshou, he was granted rank as marquis within the passes and a hundred pounds of gold.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 3 (120 BCE) he received the rank of marquis within the passes and a hundred pounds of gold.

sentences.zh: 明年，為右將軍，從大將軍出定襄，迷失道，當斬，贖為庶人。

translations.literal: The next year he was right general, followed the great general out of Dingxiang, lost the way, was liable for execution, and ransomed his life to become a commoner.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, as right general on the great general’s Dingxiang expedition, he went astray, faced capital sentence, and ransomed down to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 郭昌，雲中人，以校尉從大將軍。

translations.literal: Guo Chang was a man of Yunzhong; as colonel he followed the great general.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Chang of Yunzhong served under the great general as a colonel.

sentences.zh: 元封四年，以太中大夫為拔胡將軍，屯朔方。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yuanfeng, as grand counselor of the palace he became general for subduing the Hu and encamped at Shuofang.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Yuanfeng (107 BCE) he was promoted from grand counselor to general for subduing the Hu and took up station at Shuofang.

sentences.zh: 還擊昆明，無功，奪印。

translations.literal: He returned to strike Kunming, without merit, and his seal was taken.
translations.idiomatic: Recalled to campaign against Kunming, he accomplished nothing and had his general’s seal taken away.

sentences.zh: 荀彘，太原廣武人，以御見，侍中，用校尉數從大將軍。

translations.literal: Xun Zhi was a man of Guangwu in Taiyuan; through charioteering he was presented, became palace attendant, and as colonel several times followed the great general.
translations.idiomatic: Xun Zhi of Guangwu in Taiyuan entered court as an imperial driver, rose to palace attendant, and repeatedly campaigned as a colonel under the great general.

sentences.zh: 元封三年，為左將軍擊朝鮮，無功，坐捕樓船將軍誅。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yuanfeng, as left general he struck Joseon, without merit, and for arresting the tower-ship general was executed.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanfeng 3 (108 BCE) he commanded the left wing against Joseon without distinction and was put to death for seizing the tower-ship general.

sentences.zh: 最票騎將軍去病凡六出擊匈奴，其四出以將軍，斬首虜十一萬餘級。

translations.literal: In sum Swift-cavalry General Qubing six times went out to strike the Xiongnu; four times he went out as commanding general and beheaded or captured more than 110,000 grades.
translations.idiomatic: Swift-cavalry general Huo Qubing campaigned against the Xiongnu six times, four of them as supreme commander, and accounted for more than one hundred ten thousand heads and prisoners.

sentences.zh: 渾邪王以眾降數萬，開河西酒泉之地，西方益少胡寇。

translations.literal: King Hunyex with the masses who surrendered numbered several tens of thousands; he opened the land of Hexi and Jiuquan on the west, and on the western side Hu raiding gradually grew less.
translations.idiomatic: King Hunyex’s defection brought tens of thousands of followers, opened Hexi and Jiuquan to Han settlement, and from then on western marauders grew scarce.

sentences.zh: 四益封，凡萬七千七百戶。

translations.literal: Four times he received additions to his fief, in all 17,700 households.
translations.idiomatic: His fief was enlarged four times, for a total of seventeen thousand seven hundred households.

sentences.zh: 其校尉吏有功侯者六人，為將軍者二人。

translations.literal: Of his colonels and officers who had merit and were enfeoffed as marquises there were six; those who became generals were two.
translations.idiomatic: Six colonels and officers under him won marquisates for merit; two rose to general’s rank.

sentences.zh: 路博德

translations.literal: Lu Bode (subsection heading).
translations.idiomatic: The following subsection treats Lu Bode.

sentences.zh: 路博德，西河平州人，以右北平太守從票騎將軍，封邳離侯。

translations.literal: Lu Bode was a man of Pingzhou in West River; as prefect of Youbeiping he followed the swift-cavalry general and was enfeoffed as marquis of Pilü.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Bode of Pingzhou in West River followed the swift-cavalry general as prefect of Youbeiping and was invested as marquis of Pilü.

sentences.zh: 票騎死後，博德以衛尉為伏波將軍，伐破南越，益封。

translations.literal: After the swift-cavalry general died, Bode as commandant of the guards became wave-quelling general, attacked and broke the Southern Yue, and received an addition to his fief.
translations.idiomatic: After Qubing’s death Lu Bode, as commandant of the guards, took the wave-quelling general’s baton, crushed the Southern Yue, and gained a larger fief.

sentences.zh: 其後坐法失侯。

translations.literal: Afterward he lost his marquisate sitting for violating the law.
translations.idiomatic: He later lost his title to a legal conviction.

sentences.zh: 為彊弩都尉，屯居延，卒。

translations.literal: He became strong crossbow commandant, encamped at Juyan, and died.
translations.idiomatic: He ended his career as strong-crossbow commandant stationed at Juyan, where he died.

sentences.zh: 趙破奴

translations.literal: Zhao Ponu (subsection heading).
translations.idiomatic: The following subsection treats Zhao Ponu.

sentences.zh: 趙破奴，太原人。

translations.literal: Zhao Ponu was a man of Taiyuan.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Ponu came from Taiyuan.

sentences.zh: 嘗亡入匈奴，已而歸漢，為票騎將軍司馬。

translations.literal: He once fled into the Xiongnu, afterward returned to Han and became major to the swift-cavalry general.
translations.idiomatic: He had once run off to the Xiongnu, then come back to serve Han as the swift-cavalry general’s chief of staff.

sentences.zh: 出北地，封從票侯，坐酎金失侯。

translations.literal: He marched out of Beidi and was enfeoffed as marquis attendant on the swift-cavalry; he lost his marquisate sitting for the libation gold offense.
translations.idiomatic: He earned the title marquis attendant on the swift-cavalry column from the Beidi campaign, then lost it in the communal-wine-gold inquest.

sentences.zh: 後一歲，為匈河將軍，攻胡至匈河水，無功。

translations.literal: One year later he became Xiongnu River general, attacked the Hu as far as the Xiongnu River—without merit.
translations.idiomatic: A year later he led the Xiongnu River expedition deep into barbarian territory without notable success.

sentences.zh: 後一歲，擊虜樓蘭王，後為浞野侯。

translations.literal: One year later he struck and captured the king of Loulan, afterward becoming marquis of Zhuoye.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he captured the king of Loulan and was later enfeoffed as marquis of Zhuoye.

sentences.zh: 後六歲，以浚稽將軍將二萬騎擊匈奴左王。

translations.literal: Six years later, as Juji general he led twenty thousand horse to strike the Xiongnu left king.
translations.idiomatic: Six years after that, as Juji general, he took twenty thousand cavalry against the Xiongnu left king.

sentences.zh: 左王與戰，兵八萬騎圍破奴，破奴為虜所得，遂沒其軍。

translations.literal: The left king fought him; with eighty thousand horse he surrounded Ponu; Ponu was taken by the enemy, and thus his army was swallowed.
translations.idiomatic: The left king met him with eighty thousand riders, surrounded him, took him alive, and annihilated his command.

sentences.zh: 居匈奴中十歲，復與其太子安國亡入漢。

translations.literal: He dwelt among the Xiongnu ten years, then again with his heir apparent Anguo fled into Han.
translations.idiomatic: He spent ten years in captivity, then escaped back to Han with his heir Anguo.

sentences.zh: 後坐巫蠱，族。

translations.literal: Afterward he sat for witchcraft and sorcery—his clan was exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: He was later caught in the witchcraft prosecutions and his whole clan was executed.

sentences.zh: 自衛氏興，大將軍青首封，其後支屬五人為侯。

translations.literal: From the rise of the Wei clan, Great General Qing was first enfeoffed; afterward collateral kin five persons became marquises.
translations.idiomatic: When the Wei house rose to power, the great general was the first ennobled; five more collateral kinsmen later received marquisates.

sentences.zh: 凡二十四歲而五侯皆奪國。

translations.literal: In all twenty-four years, and the five marquises all had their states taken away.
translations.idiomatic: Within twenty-four years every one of those five marquisates had been stripped away.

sentences.zh: 征和中，戾太子敗，衛氏遂滅。

translations.literal: In the Zhenghe era Crown Prince Ju was defeated, and the Wei clan was then extinguished.
translations.idiomatic: In the Zhenghe era Crown Prince Ju’s plot collapsed, and the Wei house was wiped out.

sentences.zh: 而霍去病弟光貴盛，自有傳。

translations.literal: But Huo Qubing’s younger brother Guang rose to glory; he has his own biography.
translations.idiomatic: Huo Qubing’s younger brother Guang, however, climbed to supreme power—a life told in a separate chapter.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：蘇建嘗說責「大將軍至尊重，而天下之賢士大夫無稱焉，願將軍觀古名將所招選者，勉之哉！」

translations.literal: The appraisement says: Su Jian once remonstrated, “The great general has reached the utmost in honor and dignity, yet worthy gentlemen of the realm do not speak his praise; I hope the general will consider whom the famous generals of antiquity recruited and selected—apply yourself to this!”
translations.idiomatic: The historian’s judgment records that Su Jian once urged him: “You stand at the summit of rank and favor, yet the empire’s best men do not celebrate your name. Study how the great captains of old chose their followers—and strive to match them.”

sentences.zh: 青謝曰：「自魏其、武安之厚賓客，天子常切齒，彼親待士大夫，招賢黜不肖者，人主之柄也。

translations.literal: Qing declined, saying, “Since the Marquises of Weiqi and Wu’an treated their retainers lavishly, the Son of Heaven has constantly ground his teeth; for kinsmen to treat scholars personally, recruit the worthy, and dismiss the unworthy—that is the ruler’s prerogative.
translations.idiomatic: Qing answered, “Ever since Wei Qi and Tian Fen showered favors on their clients, the throne has looked on such patronage with gritted teeth. Deciding whom to advance and whom to cast aside belongs to the sovereign alone.

sentences.zh: 人臣奉法遵職而已，何與招士！」

translations.literal: A minister only upholds the law and obeys his duties—what has it to do with recruiting gentlemen!”
translations.idiomatic: A subject’s duty is to obey statute and office—what business have I recruiting clients?”

sentences.zh: 票騎亦方此意，為將如此。

translations.literal: The swift-cavalry general likewise held this meaning; as a general he was thus.
translations.idiomatic: The swift-cavalry general took the same view; both men commanded in that spirit.
